import { Map, Swords, Trophy, Target, Shield, Sparkles } from 'lucide-react'
import { Callout } from '@/components/mdx/Callout'
import { Card, CardGrid } from '@/components/mdx/CardGrid'
import { Checklist } from '@/components/mdx/Checklist'
import { FAQ } from '@/components/mdx/FAQ'
import { Steps, Step } from '@/components/mdx/Steps'
import { Section } from '@/components/mdx/Section'
import { YouTubeEmbed } from '@/components/mdx/YouTubeEmbed'

export const metadata = {
  title: "anime tactical simulator how to get gems: Complete Farming Guide 2026",
  description: "Master the best ways to earn gems in Anime Tactical Simulator. Our 2026 guide covers codes, tower rewards, NPC quests, and efficient spending strategies.",
  category: "Guide",
  image: "https://placehold.co/800x400/1e293b/fff?text=Anime+Tactical+Simulator+Gems+Guide",
  date: "2026-03-04",
  author: "Anime Tactical Simulator Wiki Team"
}

<Callout type="info" title="Quick Guide">
- **anime tactical simulator how to get gems**: Primary sources include the Tower, NPC quests, and active redemption codes.
- **Tower Progression**: Earn 50 gems per standard room and a massive 500 gems every fifth room.
- **NPC Quests**: Most world bosses and side NPCs offer 500 gems per completed objective.
- **Code Redemption**: New players can instantly claim over 3,000 gems using active March 2026 codes.
- **Spending Priority**: Invest gems into "+2 Equips" and "Card Access" to maximize long-term efficiency.
</Callout>

In the competitive landscape of Roblox anime games, understanding **anime tactical simulator how to get gems** is the definitive factor between a struggling beginner and a top-tier leaderboard contender. Gems serve as the premium lifeblood of your progression, allowing you to unlock essential game passes, roll for high-tier talents, and acquire the tactical units necessary for end-game raids. Whether you are a free-to-play enthusiast or looking to optimize your resource management, this guide provides a comprehensive breakdown of every viable gem-farming method available in 2026. From the vertical challenges of the Tower to the hidden rewards in the Solo Leveling and Demon Slayer worlds, we will explore how to maximize your income. By following these professional strategies, you will ensure your squad is always equipped with the best traits and artifacts the game has to offer.

<Section icon={Sparkles} title="Active Codes and Instant Rewards">

The fastest method for **anime tactical simulator how to get gems** when starting your journey is utilizing the active redemption system. The developers frequently release codes to celebrate milestones, such as reaching one million visits or fixing major bugs. These rewards provide a significant boost that should be used primarily to unlock permanent utility upgrades rather than temporary potions.

**Video Highlights:**
- Detailed walkthrough of the Tower system and gem scaling.
- How to use codes to jumpstart your unit collection.
- Breakdown of the best game passes to buy with free gems.
- Tips for progressing through the One Piece and Demon Slayer worlds.

<YouTubeEmbed videoId="q3X3ieusO9A" title="Best Beginners Guide For Anime Tactical Simulator" />

| Code | Gem Reward | Additional Items |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **1MVISITLETSGOO** | 250 Gems | 3 Spin Wheels, 2 Keys |
| **Justkidding_thisisrealcode** | 250 Gems | 5 Spin Wheels |
| **NoMoreBugsPls** | 250 Gems | 1 Potion Bundle, 5 Keycards |
| **Sorry4Delay** | 250 Gems | 2 Dungeon Keys, All Potions |
| **ATSForever** | 500 Gems | 10 Premium Rerolls, Potions |
| **RELEASED!** | 500 Gems | 10 Talent Rerolls, Potions |

<Callout type="success" title="Redemption Tip">
Before attempting to redeem these codes, ensure you have joined the Tsukikage Roblox community group. Most high-value codes are locked behind group membership to prevent botting and reward loyal players.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Trophy} title="The Tower and Achievement System">

For consistent, long-term gem income, the Tower stands as the most reliable mechanic. Unlike one-time codes, the Tower offers scaling rewards as you climb higher, testing both your tactical placement and unit strength. Each room provides a baseline of 50 gems, but the real value lies in the milestone floors.

| Milestone | Gem Reward | Bonus Items |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Standard Room | 50 Gems | Random Gold |
| Every 5th Room | 500 Gems | Potion Bundles |
| Every 10th Room | 500 Gems | Talent Dices/Keys |
| High Floor Bonus | 1,000+ Gems | Mythic Souls |

Beyond the Tower, the achievement menu offers massive lump sums of gems for completing specific gameplay milestones. High-tier achievements, particularly those involving "Star Opening" and "Enemy Defeats," often reward 1,000 gems per chest.

<Callout type="info" title="Achievement Focus">
Prioritize the "Time Achievements" early on. These grant luck boosts, which help you pull Secret and Tactical units faster. Better units allow you to clear higher Tower floors, creating a positive feedback loop for your gem economy.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Map} title="World Progression and NPC Quests">

Each new world you unlock—from the starting Dragon Ball area to the endgame Solo Leveling zone—is populated with NPCs who offer specific quests. These are not just for experience; they are a primary answer for **anime tactical simulator how to get gems**. Most major NPCs like Goku, Luffy, and Giyu provide 500 gems upon completion of their primary objectives.

<CardGrid cols={3}>
  <Card title="Early Game (DBZ/OP)">
    - **Goku Quests**: 500 Gems
    - **Luffy/Usopp**: 500 Gems each
    - Focus on full Legendary teams
  </Card>
  <Card title="Mid Game (Demon Slayer)">
    - **Giyu Quests**: 500 Gems
    - **Boss Raids**: Gem Tokens
    - Farm Kokushibo/Yoriichi
  </Card>
  <Card title="End Game (Solo Leveling)">
    - **Jin Wu Quests**: 500 Gems
    - **Egress Raid**: Secret Drops
    - Switch stats to Pure Damage
  </Card>
</CardGrid>

<Callout type="tip" title="Side Boss Strategy">
Don't ignore side bosses like Majimu in the later worlds. These NPCs often provide Dragon Balls or unique accessories alongside gems, which are essential for the Shenron wish system.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Target} title="Strategic Spending and Game Passes">

Acquiring gems is only half the battle; spending them correctly is what separates elite players. In Anime Tactical Simulator, you can purchase "Game Passes" using your farmed gems. This is a rare feature that allows free-to-play players to access premium power.

| Priority | Game Pass | Gem Cost | Benefit |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Essential** | +2 Unit Equips | 3,000 - 5,000 | Increases total DPS significantly |
| **High** | Card Access | 4,000 - 6,000 | Allows background card rolling |
| **Medium** | 2x Gold | 5,000+ | Faster Yen farming for upgrades |
| **Luxury** | Fast Rolls | 10,000+ | Quality of life for end-game |

<Callout type="warning" title="Avoid Early Spending">
Resist the urge to spend gems on temporary Potions or Talent Dices in the early game. Save every gem until you can afford the "+2 Unit Equips" pass, as this provides the single largest boost to your progression speed.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Shield} title="Optimized Daily Routine for Gems">

To maintain a steady flow of resources, you should establish a daily routine that maximizes time-to-gem efficiency. Following a structured path ensures you don't waste time on low-yield activities.

<Steps>
  <Step num="1" title="Check for New Codes">
    Always start your session by checking official social media or community Wikis for new codes to get an immediate gem injection.
  </Step>
  <Step num="2" title="Daily Tower Climb">
    Push at least 5 to 10 floors in the Tower. The 500-gem bonus every fifth floor is the most time-efficient farming method in the game.
  </Step>
  <Step num="3" title="Clear World Quests">
    Travel to your highest unlocked world and ensure all NPC quests are complete. If you've just reached a new world, prioritize the 500-gem NPC objectives first.
  </Step>
  <Step num="4" title="Spin the Wheel">
    Utilize your free spin every 30 minutes. While rare, the wheel can drop gem bundles and high-tier potions that save you gems later.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Checklist
  id="gem-milestones"
  title="Progression Goals for 2026:"
  items={[
    "Redeem all active March 2026 codes",
    "Reach Floor 50 in the Tower for milestone rewards",
    "Unlock the +2 Unit Equips game pass",
    "Complete all NPC quests in the Demon Slayer world",
    "Obtain a full team of Tactical units for faster farming"
  ]}
/>

<Callout type="tip" title="Luck Synergy">
Equip units with the "Fortune" or "God's Blessing" traits when farming bosses. While this doesn't directly increase gem drops, it increases the chance of getting rare units and accessories, which you would otherwise have to spend gems to roll for.
</Callout>

</Section>

<Section icon={Swords} title="Frequently Asked Questions">

<FAQ items={[
  {
    question: "What is the fastest way for a beginner in anime tactical simulator how to get gems?",
    answer: "The absolute fastest way is to redeem all active codes, which can provide over 3,000 gems instantly. After that, focus on completing the initial NPC quests in the Dragon Ball world."
  },
  {
    question: "Are there any infinite gem glitches in 2026?",
    answer: "No, there are no legitimate infinite gem glitches. Beware of scams claiming otherwise. The most efficient legal 'infinite' method is climbing the Tower and completing repeatable achievements."
  },
  {
    question: "Should I spend gems on Talent Rerolls or Game Passes?",
    answer: "Always prioritize Game Passes first. Permanent upgrades like extra unit slots or background rolling provide much more value than a single talent reroll, which is RNG-dependent."
  },
  {
    question: "How many gems do the Demon Slayer boss fights give?",
    answer: "While the bosses themselves primarily drop tokens and units, the associated quests and achievements for defeating them reward 500 to 1,000 gems respectively."
  }
]} />

</Section>
